items.q: What is the worst-case complexity of bubble sort?
items.misconception: O(n) because it just passes through the list.
items.why: Students count one pass and forget that bubble sort does up to n passes, each comparing up to n elements. The nested loop structure gives n times n = n squared.

items.q: What is the scope of a local variable?
items.correct: Accessible only within the function/block where it is declared.
items.random_wrong: Stored on a local computer rather than a server.
items.why: Students confuse 'local' with 'on this computer'. In programming, scope refers to the CODE REGION where the variable name resolves - local variables vanish when the function returns.

items.q: Why must a recursive function have a base case?
items.correct: Without it, the function calls itself indefinitely, causing a stack overflow.
items.random_wrong: To make the function run faster.
items.why: Students treat the base case as optional ceremony. It's the TERMINATION CONDITION that stops recursion - each call adds a stack frame, and without termination the stack will overflow.

items.q: Which storage has no moving parts?
items.why: It's SSD. “HDD” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Internet access. “A printer” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many copies are recommended in the '3-2-1' backup rule (total)?
items.why: The real figure is 3. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
